name: HMIS Investigator (J 81)
events: 16 Oct 1940 Around 0715 EF/16, HMS Hector (Capt.(Retd.) R.W.M. Lloyd, DSO, RN) departed Bombay to search for the merchant vessel Marly (Norwegian, 1115 GRT, built 1918) which had sent an SOS from position 18°30'N, 72°21'E stating they had encountered a cyclone and were sinking and taking to the lifeboats.
The sloop HMIS Investigator (Lt. W.J.G. Hughes, RIN), which was out on patrol, was also sent to search for the survivors but she was forced to return due to the heavy weather.
The Norwegian ship was not encountered and no survivors were ever found. (1)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
